page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. The system features 384 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It includes 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 909.18 GB/sec for large block reads and 403.59 GB/sec for writes. Database and web serving capabilities are also highly rated, with Redis SET operations achieving 383.07 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 144.2 GB/sec.

While multi-core and parallelized tasks perform exceptionally well, single-core execution is average, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores. Additionally, LLM text generation performance is a notable bottleneck, dropping to a poor 2.31 tokens/sec for large 70B models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density and exceptional throughput per unit of hardware, making it highly efficient for highly parallelized workloads, though less optimal for sequential or heavy AI text generation tasks.
This server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive parallel processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 384 GB of DDR5 RAM and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, this instance delivers high-throughput performance. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU execution, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operations, including 383.07 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance remains average, and LLM text generation for large models is weak. Qualitatively, the server provides excellent cost efficiency for highly parallelized, storage-heavy workloads due to its massive core density and fast local storage. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, high-volume database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11.4 TB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-48xl features 192 Graviton cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11.4 TB NVMe SSD for high-throughput compute and database tasks.
tagline: A high-density, bare-metal ARMv9 server featuring 192 dedicated cores and massive local NVMe storage for parallelized, high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.7 GHz
384 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database throughput
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-speed data transfer
Average single-core performance and limited LLM text generation speeds
Ideal for software compilation, high-throughput databases, and static web serving
